raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "defensive assertion or rebuttal that equates naturalness with moral acceptability",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "a meaningful distinction exists between what is 'according to nature' and what is not",
    "moral value (good/evil) can be determined by conformity to nature",
    "nature functions as a normative or authoritative standard"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent as a definitional axiom but risks circularity unless 'nature' and 'evil' receive independent, non-moral definitions",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "shared acceptance of natural-law or teleological ethics in which conformity to nature reliably tracks moral goodness",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "use of the claim to justify harmful behaviors or outcomes merely because they occur in nature (appeal-to-nature fallacy)"
}
